description:
Find all references to a C# symbol across the workspace using Roslyn semantic analysis (same engine as Visual Studio). Unlike grep, results are semantically exact: overloads, partial classes, generics and using-aliases are resolved, and comment/string mentions are excluded.

Point at the symbol by giving a file, a 1-based line number, and the identifier text on that line (e.g. the declaration line of a class/method/field, or any usage site). Returns matching locations grouped by file with line numbers and source text.

Notes:
- Code references only. Unity serialized references (UnityEvent bindings, m_Script GUIDs in scenes/prefabs) are NOT included; use unity_ref_search for asset-level references.
- The C# analysis server warms up on first use (it may download components and load the solution). If the tool reports it is still preparing, retry after a moment.

file_path.description: Path to the .cs file containing the symbol, relative to the workspace root (e.g. 'Assets/Scripts/Player.cs') or absolute.

line.description: 1-based line number where the symbol appears.

symbol.description: The identifier on that line to query (e.g. 'PlayerController', 'TakeDamage'). Must match a whole identifier; the first whole-word occurrence on the line is used.

include_declaration.description: Include the declaration itself in results. Default false.
